name: peppermint
mechanism: Menthol-rich peppermint oil can relax gastrointestinal smooth muscle via calcium-channel effects and TRPM8 signaling.
interactions: Monitor with acid-suppressing therapy because enteric-coated capsule release can be altered
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated. May cause heartburn or reflux, nausea or mouth or perianal burning with some formulations. Avoid concentrated oil in infants and young children; Use caution with severe GERD because reflux can worsen; Use caution with gallbladder disease unless advised by a clinician; Avoid concentrated oil in infants and young children, heartburn or reflux, nausea or mouth or perianal burning with some formulations; Avoid combining with prescription drugs without guidance.

sources.title: PubMed search: peppermint
sources.note: Literature index for pharmacology and safety evidence

description: Peppermint is a medicinal herb traditionally used for post-meal comfort tea use and low-friction single-herb daily beverage routines.
duration: Often used as-needed around meals or in short daily routines.
categoryUseContext: Common in digestive comfort plans, post-meal tea habits, and simple single-herb beverage routines.
evidenceLevel: Moderate traditional use support with selected clinical evidence depending on form (tea vs oil/capsule).
